docs.title: gocam
docs.text:
Gene Ontology Causal Activity Model (GO-CAM) Schema.

This schema provides a way of representing causal pathway Models. A model consists of a set of Activity objects, where each activity object represents the function of either an individual gene product, a protein complex of gene products, or a set of possible gene products.

Each Models has associated metadata slots. Some slots such as id, title, and status are required.

URI: https://w3id.org/gocam

Name: gocam

EnabledByGeneProductAssociation An association between an activity and an individual gene product EnabledByProteinComplexAssociation An association between an activity and a protein complex, where the complex c... MoleculeAssociation An association between an activity and a molecule node TermAssociation An association between an activity and a term, potentially with extensions BiologicalProcessAssociation An association between an activity and a biological process term CellTypeAssociation An association between an activity and a cell type term CellularAnatomicalEntityAssociation An association between an activity and a cellular anatomical entity term GrossAnatomyAssociation An association between an activity and a gross anatomical structure term MolecularFunctionAssociation An association between an activity and a molecular function term PartOfProteinComplexAssociation An association between a gene product and a protein complex that it is a part... PhaseAssociation An association between an activity or biological process and a phase term ProteinComplexHasPartAssociation An association between a protein complex and one of its parts EvidenceItem An individual piece of evidence that is associated with an assertion in a mod... Model A model of a biological program consisting of a set of causally connected act... MoleculeNode An individual molecule or chemical entity in a causal model, representing a s... Object An abstract class for all identified objects in a model PublicationObject An object that represents a publication or other kind of reference TermObject An abstract class for all ontology term objects BiologicalProcessTermObject A term object that represents a biological process term from GO CellTypeTermObject A term object that represents a cell type term from CL CellularAnatomicalEntityTermObject A term object that represents a cellular anatomical entity term from GO EvidenceTermObject A term object that represents an evidence term from ECO GrossAnatomicalStructureTermObject A term object that represents a gross anatomical structure term from UBERON InformationBiomacromoleculeTermObject An abstract class for all information biomacromolecule term objects GeneProductTermObject A term object that represents a gene product term from GO or UniProtKB ProteinComplexTermObject A term object that represents a protein complex term from GO MolecularFunctionTermObject A term object that represents a molecular function term from GO MoleculeTermObject A term object that represents a molecule term from CHEBI or UniProtKB PhaseTermObject A term object that represents a phase term from GO or UBERON PredicateTermObject A term object that represents an OWL relation from the OBO Relations Ontology TaxonTermObject A term object that represents a taxon term from NCBITaxon ProvenanceInfo Provenance information for an object QueryIndex An index that is optionally placed on a model in order to support common quer...

docs.title: Enumerations
docs.text: Enumeration Description CausalPredicateEnum A term describing the causal relationship between two activities CellularAnatomicalEntityEnum A term from the subset of the cellular anatomical entity branch of GO CC EvidenceCodeEnum A term from the subset of ECO that maps up to a GAF evidence code InformationBiomacromoleculeCategory A term describing the type of the enabler of an activity ModelStateEnum A term describing where the model is in the development life cycle

docs.text: Type Description Boolean A binary (true or false) value Curie a compact URI Date a date (year, month and day) in an idealized calendar DateOrDatetime Either a date or a datetime Datetime The combination of a date and time Decimal A real number with arbitrary precision that conforms to the xsd:decimal speci... Double A real number that conforms to the xsd:double specification Float A real number that conforms to the xsd:float specification Integer An integer Jsonpath A string encoding a JSON Path Jsonpointer A string encoding a JSON Pointer Ncname Prefix part of CURIE Nodeidentifier A URI, CURIE or BNODE that represents a node in a model Objectidentifier A URI or CURIE that represents an object in the model Sparqlpath A string encoding a SPARQL Property Path String A character string Time A time object represents a (local) time of day, independent of any particular... Uri a complete URI Uriorcurie a URI or a CURIE

docs.title: Subsets
docs.text: Subset Description

docs.title: Class: Activity
docs.text: An individual activity in a causal model, representing the individual molecular activity of a single gene product or complex in the context of a particular model

docs.title: Class: Association
docs.text: An abstract grouping for different kinds of evidence-associated provenance

docs.title: Class: BiologicalProcessAssociation
docs.text: An association between an activity and a biological process term

docs.title: Class: BiologicalProcessTermObject
docs.text: A term object that represents a biological process term from GO

docs.title: Boolean
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Boolean

A binary (true or false) value

docs.title: Class: CausalAssociation
docs.text: A causal association between two activities

docs.title: Enum: CausalPredicateEnum
docs.text: A term describing the causal relationship between two activities. All terms are drawn from the "causally upstream or within" (RO:0002418) branch of the Relation Ontology (RO).

docs.title: Class: CellTypeAssociation
docs.text: An association between an activity and a cell type term

docs.title: Class: CellTypeTermObject
docs.text: A term object that represents a cell type term from CL

docs.title: Class: CellularAnatomicalEntityAssociation
docs.text: An association between an activity and a cellular anatomical entity term

docs.title: Enum: CellularAnatomicalEntityEnum
docs.text: A term from the subset of the cellular anatomical entity branch of GO CC

docs.title: Class: CellularAnatomicalEntityTermObject
docs.text: A term object that represents a cellular anatomical entity term from GO

docs.title: Curie
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Curie

a compact URI

docs.title: Date
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Date

a date (year, month and day) in an idealized calendar

docs.title: DateOrDatetime
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: DateOrDatetime

Either a date or a datetime

docs.title: Datetime
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Datetime

The combination of a date and time

docs.title: Decimal
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Decimal

A real number with arbitrary precision that conforms to the xsd:decimal specification

docs.title: Double
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Double

A real number that conforms to the xsd:double specification

docs.title: Class: EnabledByAssociation
docs.text:
An association between an activity and the gene product or complex or set of potential gene products

_ that carry out that activity._

docs.title: Class: EnabledByGeneProductAssociation
docs.text: An association between an activity and an individual gene product

docs.title: Class: EnabledByProteinComplexAssociation
docs.text: An association between an activity and a protein complex, where the complex carries out the activity. This should only be used when the activity cannot be attributed to an individual member of the complex, but instead the function is an emergent property of the complex.

docs.title: Enum: EvidenceCodeEnum
docs.text: A term from the subset of ECO that maps up to a GAF evidence code

docs.title: Class: EvidenceItem
docs.text: An individual piece of evidence that is associated with an assertion in a model

docs.title: Class: EvidenceTermObject
docs.text: A term object that represents an evidence term from ECO. Only ECO terms that map up to a GO GAF evidence code should be used.

docs.title: Float
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Float

A real number that conforms to the xsd:float specification

docs.title: Class: GeneProductTermObject
docs.text: A term object that represents a gene product term from GO or UniProtKB

docs.title: Class: GrossAnatomicalStructureTermObject
docs.text: A term object that represents a gross anatomical structure term from UBERON

docs.title: Class: GrossAnatomyAssociation
docs.text: An association between an activity and a gross anatomical structure term

docs.title: Enum: InformationBiomacromoleculeCategory
docs.text: A term describing the type of the enabler of an activity.

docs.title: Class: InformationBiomacromoleculeTermObject
docs.text: An abstract class for all information biomacromolecule term objects

docs.title: Integer
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Integer

An integer

docs.title: Jsonpath
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Jsonpath

A string encoding a JSON Path. The value of the string MUST conform to JSON Point syntax and SHOULD dereference to zero or more valid objects within the current instance document when encoded in tree form.

docs.title: Jsonpointer
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Jsonpointer

A string encoding a JSON Pointer. The value of the string MUST conform to JSON Point syntax and SHOULD dereference to a valid object within the current instance document when encoded in tree form.

docs.title: Class: Model
docs.text: A model of a biological program consisting of a set of causally connected activities.

docs.title: Enum: ModelStateEnum
docs.text: A term describing where the model is in the development life cycle.

docs.title: Class: MolecularFunctionAssociation
docs.text: An association between an activity and a molecular function term

docs.title: Class: MolecularFunctionTermObject
docs.text: A term object that represents a molecular function term from GO

docs.title: Class: MoleculeAssociation
docs.text: An association between an activity and a molecule node

docs.title: Class: MoleculeNode
docs.text: An individual molecule or chemical entity in a causal model, representing a specific occurrence of a molecule that participates in activities as an input or output and may be localized to a particular cellular location.

docs.title: Class: MoleculeTermObject
docs.text: A term object that represents a molecule term from CHEBI or UniProtKB

docs.title: Ncname
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Ncname

Prefix part of CURIE

docs.title: Nodeidentifier
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Nodeidentifier

A URI, CURIE or BNODE that represents a node in a model.

docs.title: Class: Object
docs.text: An abstract class for all identified objects in a model

docs.title: Objectidentifier
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Objectidentifier

A URI or CURIE that represents an object in the model.

docs.title: Class: PartOfProteinComplexAssociation
docs.text: An association between a gene product and a protein complex that it is a part of.

docs.title: Class: PhaseAssociation
docs.text: An association between an activity or biological process and a phase term

docs.title: Class: PhaseTermObject
docs.text: A term object that represents a phase term from GO or UBERON

docs.title: Class: PredicateTermObject
docs.text: A term object that represents an OWL relation from the OBO Relations Ontology

docs.title: Class: ProteinComplexHasPartAssociation
docs.text: An association between a protein complex and one of its parts.

docs.title: Class: ProteinComplexTermObject
docs.text: A term object that represents a protein complex term from GO

docs.title: Class: ProvenanceInfo
docs.text: Provenance information for an object

docs.title: Class: PublicationObject
docs.text: An object that represents a publication or other kind of reference

docs.title: Class: QueryIndex
docs.text: An index that is optionally placed on a model in order to support common query or index operations. Note that this index is not typically populated in the working transactional store for a model, it is derived via computation from core primary model information.

docs.title: Sparqlpath
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Sparqlpath

A string encoding a SPARQL Property Path. The value of the string MUST conform to SPARQL syntax and SHOULD dereference to zero or more valid objects within the current instance document when encoded as RDF.

docs.title: String
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: String

A character string

docs.title: Class: TaxonTermObject
docs.text: A term object that represents a taxon term from NCBITaxon

docs.title: Class: TermAssociation
docs.text: An association between an activity and a term, potentially with extensions. This is an abstract class for grouping purposes, it should not be directly instantiated, instead a subclass should be instantiated.

docs.title: Class: TermObject
docs.text: An abstract class for all ontology term objects

docs.title: Time
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Time

A time object represents a (local) time of day, independent of any particular day

docs.title: Uri
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Uri

a complete URI

docs.title: Uriorcurie
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Uriorcurie

a URI or a CURIE

docs.title: gocam
docs.text: GO CAM Data Model (Python)

docs.title: Slot: activities
docs.text: All of the activities that are part of the model

docs.title: Slot: additional_taxa
docs.text: Additional taxa that the model is about

docs.title: Slot: causal_associations
docs.text: The causal associations that flow out of this activity

docs.title: Slot: comments
docs.text: Curator-provided comments about the model

docs.title: Slot: date_modified
docs.text: The date that the model was last modified

docs.title: Slot: downstream_activity
docs.text: The activity unit that is downstream of this one

docs.title: Slot: enabled_by
docs.text: The gene product or complex that carries out the activity

docs.title: Slot: end_activities
docs.text: The set of activities that are the end points of the model, i.e. those that have no outgoing causal associations.

docs.title: Slot: evidence
docs.text: The set of evidence items that support the association.

docs.title: Slot: flattened_contributors
docs.text: All contributor values from the model across different levels combined in one place

docs.title: Slot: flattened_evidence_terms
docs.text: All evidence term objects from the model across different levels combined in one place

docs.title: Slot: flattened_provided_by
docs.text: All provided_by values from the model across different levels combined in one place

docs.title: Slot: flattened_references
docs.text: All publication objects from the model across different levels combined in one place

docs.title: gocam
docs.text:
Gene Ontology Causal Activity Model (GO-CAM) Schema.

This schema provides a way of representing causal pathway Models. A model consists of a set of Activity objects, where each activity object represents the function of either an individual gene product, a protein complex of gene products, or a set of possible gene products.

Each Models has associated metadata slots. Some slots such as id, title, and status are required.

URI: https://w3id.org/gocam

docs.title: Slot: intermediate_activities
docs.text: The set of activities that are neither start nor end activities, i.e. those that have both incoming and outgoing causal associations.

docs.title: Slot: length_of_longest_causal_association_path
docs.text: The maximum number of hops along activities along the direction of causal flow in a model.

docs.title: Slot: located_in
docs.text: The cellular location in which the molecule is located

docs.title: Slot: model_activity_enabled_by_closure
docs.text: The reflexive transitive closure of model_activity_enabled_by_terms, over the is_a and part_of relationship types

docs.title: Slot: model_activity_enabled_by_genes
docs.text: All direct enabler genes and genes that are part of enabler complexes for all activities

docs.title: Slot: model_activity_enabled_by_rollup
docs.text: The rollup of model_activity_enabled_by_closure to a GO subset or slim.

docs.title: Slot: model_activity_enabled_by_terms
docs.text: All direct enabler terms for all activities

docs.title: Slot: model_activity_has_input_closure
docs.text: The reflexive transitive closure of model_activity_has_input_terms, over the is_a relationship type

docs.title: Slot: model_activity_has_input_rollup
docs.text: The rollup of model_activity_has_input_closure to a GO subset or slim.

docs.title: Slot: model_activity_has_input_terms
docs.text: All direct input terms for all activities

docs.title: Slot: model_activity_molecular_function_closure
docs.text: The reflexive transitive closure of model_activity_molecular_function_terms, over the is_a relationship

docs.title: Slot: model_activity_molecular_function_rollup
docs.text: The rollup of model_activity_molecular_function_closure to a GO subset or slim.

docs.title: Slot: model_activity_molecular_function_terms
docs.text: All MF terms for all activities

docs.title: Slot: model_activity_occurs_in_closure
docs.text: The reflexive transitive closure of model_activity_occurs_in_terms, over the is_a and part_of relationship type

docs.title: Slot: model_activity_occurs_in_rollup
docs.text: The rollup of model_activity_occurs_in_closure to a GO subset or slim.

docs.title: Slot: model_activity_occurs_in_terms
docs.text: All direct cellular component localization terms for all activities

docs.title: Slot: model_activity_part_of_closure
docs.text: The reflexive transitive closure of model_activity_part_of_terms, over the is_a and part_of relationship type

docs.title: Slot: model_activity_part_of_rollup
docs.text: The rollup of model_activity_part_of_closure to a GO subset or slim.

docs.title: Slot: model_activity_part_of_terms
docs.text: All direct biological process terms for all activities

docs.title: Slot: model_chemical_terms
docs.text: All molecule terms in the model that are chemical entities (e.g. substrates or products) and not information biomacromolecules (e.g. gene products or complexes).

docs.title: Slot: model_taxon
docs.text: The primary taxon term for the model, over the NCBITaxon:subClassOf relationship type. This is used to determine the primary taxon that the model is relevant to.

docs.title: Slot: model_taxon_closure
docs.text: The reflexive transitive closure of the taxon term for the model, over the NCBITaxon:subClassOf relationship type. This is used to determine the set of taxa that are relevant to the model.

docs.title: Slot: model_taxon_rollup
docs.text: The rollup of the taxon closure to a NCBITaxon subset or slim.

docs.title: Slot: molecular_associations
docs.text: Associations between the activity and molecules that are relevant to it. This includes molecules that are products/substrates or regulators of the activity.

docs.title: Slot: molecular_function
docs.text: The molecular function that is carried out by the gene product or complex

docs.title: Slot: molecule
docs.text: The molecule node that is associated with this activity

docs.title: Slot: molecules
docs.text: All of the molecule nodes that are part of the model.

docs.title: Slot: number_of_activities
docs.text: The number of activities in a model.

docs.title: Slot: number_of_causal_associations
docs.text: Total number of causal association edges connecting activities in a model. This includes both explicit causal associations (e.g. "directly positively regulates") between activities and implicit causal associations between activities with shared input/output chemical entities.

docs.title: Slot: number_of_enabled_by_terms
docs.text: The number of molecular entities or sets of entities in a model.

docs.title: Slot: number_of_end_activities
docs.text: The number of end activities in a model

docs.title: Slot: number_of_intermediate_activities
docs.text: The number of intermediate activities in a model

docs.title: Slot: number_of_singleton_activities
docs.text: The number of singleton activities in a model

docs.title: Slot: number_of_start_activities
docs.text: The number of start activities in a model

docs.title: Slot: number_of_strongly_connected_components
docs.text: The number of distinct components that consist of activities that are connected (directly or indirectly) via causal connections. Most models will consist of a single SCC. Some models may consist of two or more "islands" where there is no connection from one island to another.

docs.title: Slot: objects
docs.text: All of the objects that are part of the model. This includes terms as well as publications and database objects like gene. This is not strictly part of the data managed by the model, it is for convenience, and should be refreshed from outside.

docs.title: Slot: occurs_in
docs.text: The cellular location in which the activity occurs

docs.title: Slot: part_of
docs.text: A generic slot for part-of relationships

docs.title: Slot: provenances
docs.text: A generic slot for provenance information

docs.title: Slot: query_index
docs.text: An optional object that contains the results of indexing a model with various summary statistics and retrieval indices.

docs.title: Slot: reference
docs.text: The publication of reference that describes the evidence

docs.title: Slot: singleton_activities
docs.text: The set of activities that have no causal associations, i.e. those that are not connected to any other activity in the model.

docs.title: Slot: start_activities
docs.text: The set of activities that are the starting points of the model, i.e. those that have no incoming causal associations.

docs.title: Slot: status
docs.text: The status of the model in terms of its progression along the developmental lifecycle

docs.title: Slot: taxon
docs.text: The primary taxon that the model is about

docs.title: Slot: taxon_label
docs.text: The label of the primary taxon for the model

docs.title: Slot: term
docs.text: A generic slot for ontology terms

docs.title: Slot: title
docs.text: The human-readable descriptive title of the model

docs.title: Slot: with_objects
docs.text: Supporting database entities or terms
